 {"id":"br-2wsr","title":"Fix flaky test: http_post_base_path_without_slash_matches_base_with_slash","description":"Test in mcp-agent-mail-server/src/lib.rs fails intermittently in full suite but passes individually. Likely caused by test isolation issues - shared global state (metrics counters, logging configuration) being mutated by concurrent tests. Consider adding explicit state reset or using test isolation fixtures.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-09T05:32:39.014423948Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T05:37:42.547891577Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T05:37:42.547872421Z","close_reason":"Not a real flaky test. Failures were from multi-agent compilation races (other agents modifying source mid-build). Test passes consistently in isolation AND in full suite when compiled from stable source. Verified with 3 isolated runs + full 1148-test server suite run - all green.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-2x5p4","title":"T3.9: E2E test suite for am bench","description":"## Objective
Build E2E coverage for `am bench` to verify full operator workflows and artifact quality.

## Work
- Exercise quick/full benchmark runs, filtering, baseline save/load, and regression detection flows.
- Validate CLI UX, exit codes, and report contents in realistic environments.
- Emit reproducible logs/artifacts suitable for CI and release reviews.

## Deliverable
An E2E suite proving native benchmarking is reliable, fast, and governance-ready.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Includes comprehensive unit, integration, and end-to-end coverage for this task's behavior (happy path, failure path, and boundary conditions).
- Produces detailed, machine-readable run artifacts: structured JSON summary, per-step command traces, stdout/stderr capture, timing data, and environment metadata needed for deterministic reproduction.
- Logging is high-signal and explicit: each assertion failure reports scenario ID, input fixture, expected vs actual values, and a recommended next debug step.
- CI execution is deterministic (seed/time controls, explicit timeouts/retries, no hidden external dependencies) and failures are actionable without manual log spelunking.
- Results are wired into standard quality gates so regressions block merges.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:52:49.059705365Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T03:46:32.002367988Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2x5p4","depends_on_id":"br-2fc0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:53:17.649324398Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":260,"issue_id":"br-2x5p4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# T3.9: E2E Test Suite for `am bench`

## What to test

End-to-end validation of the `am bench` subcommand covering benchmarking,
statistics aggregation, baseline comparison, and report generation.

## Test cases

### test_bench_help_only
Run `am bench --filter "Help screen"` and verify:
- Only the "Help screen" benchmark runs
- JSON output contains the benchmark entry with times_ms array
- mean_ms, stddev_ms, p50, p95, p99 computed correctly

### test_bench_startup_category
Run `am bench --category startup` and verify:
- Only startup benchmarks run (Help screen)
- Report has correct category grouping

### test_bench_db_seeding
Run `am bench --filter "Inbox fetch"` and verify:
- DB is seeded automatically (BlueLake, RedFox, /tmp/bench, 60 messages)
- Benchmark runs successfully against seeded data
- --reseed flag forces re-seeding

### test_bench_full_run
Run `am bench` (all benchmarks) and verify:
- All 13 benchmarks execute
- JSON report written to benches/results/cli_<timestamp>.json
- Report has schema "bench_report.v1" with schema_version: 1
- hardware section has hostname, arch, kernel
- Each benchmark has: command, runs, times_ms, mean_ms, stddev_ms, p50, p95, p99, min, max

### test_bench_baseline_save_and_compare
1. Run `am bench --filter "Help screen" --save-baseline`
2. Verify baseline.json created in benches/results/
3. Run `am bench --filter "Help screen"` again
4. Verify comparison output shows delta vs baseline

### test_bench_fixture_signature
Run `am bench --filter "Help screen" --json` and verify:
- fixture_signature field present
- It's first 16 hex chars of SHA-256("Help screen|am --help||<arch>|<kernel>")

### test_bench_json_output
Run `am bench --filter "Help screen" --json` and verify stdout:
- Valid JSON output
- Contains benchmarks object with "Help screen" key
- times_ms is a non-empty array of floats

### test_bench_percentile_accuracy
Run `am bench --filter "Help screen" --runs 20 --json`:
- With 20 runs, verify p50 ≈ median, p95 ≈ 19th value, p99 ≈ 20th value
- Uses nearest-rank interpolation

## Implementation notes
- Create as tests/e2e/test_bench.sh
- Use --filter to run individual benchmarks for faster test execution
- Clean up /tmp/bench between tests for isolation
- Validate JSON structure with jq assertions
","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:53:00Z"},{"id":278,"issue_id":"br-2x5p4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## SECOND-PASS CORRECTION: T3.9 E2E test for am bench

Fix test cases based on deep audit:

1. test_bench_help_only should use --filter "help" (benchmark name derived from
   filename: help_TIMESTAMP.json → name "help"). Not "Help screen".

2. test_bench_baseline_save_and_compare: There is NO --save-baseline flag in bash.
   Baseline is set via BENCH_BASELINE_FILE env var pointing to a JSON file.
   Correct test:
   a) Run am bench --filter help and save output to /tmp/baseline.json
   b) Set BENCH_BASELINE_FILE=/tmp/baseline.json
   c) Run am bench --filter help again
   d) Verify output includes baseline_p95_ms and delta_p95_ms fields

3. test_bench_percentile_accuracy: Use round() formula, not ceil():
   idx = round(p/100 * (N-1)), clamped to [0, N-1]

4. Add test_bench_quick_mode:
   Run am bench --quick and verify warmup=1, runs=3
   (not default warmup=3, runs=10)

5. test_bench_fixture_signature should compute:
   SHA-256("help|am --help||{arch}|{kernel}")[:16]
   Note: parameters field is stringified dict, usually "{}" or empty

6. Add test_bench_conditional_skip:
   If stub encoder script doesn't exist, verify those benchmarks are skipped
   If DB seeding fails, verify operational benchmarks are skipped
   Partial results should still produce valid JSON

7. test_bench_json_output: verify schema_version is integer 1 (not string),
   verify environment_profile section exists with cwd field
","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:04:38Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-2x82","title":"T10.2: Update docs to native-first command guidance with phased compatibility notes","description":"## Objective
Cut operator/developer documentation over to native commands where parity is complete.

## Work
- Update README, OPERATOR_RUNBOOK, MIGRATION_GUIDE, RELEASE_CHECKLIST, ROLLOUT docs.
- Replace script-first examples with native `am` examples where available.
- Keep compatibility notes for workflows still in phased migration.

## Deliverable
Docs reflect actual intended command surface and migration state.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Output is self-contained: clear before/after command mapping, scope boundaries, rationale, and migration decision record.
- Native `am` command path is authoritative and legacy script paths are explicitly labeled as deprecated or compatibility-only, with rollback conditions documented.
- Operator-facing troubleshooting guidance includes where to find logs/artifacts and how to reproduce failures quickly.
- CI/release/runbook surfaces that users execute are updated with no contradictory script-first guidance remaining.
- Includes a verification checklist so future maintainers can re-audit correctness without external plan documents.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:46:58.575861676Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T02:04:50.152025069Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","migration","ops"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2x82","depends_on_id":"br-1nbs","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:47:14.711301473Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2x82","depends_on_id":"br-20qs","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:47:16.932405802Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2x82","depends_on_id":"br-246y","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:47:17.404563215Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2x82","depends_on_id":"br-2azg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:47:16.046957893Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2x82","depends_on_id":"br-39eh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:47:16.483697467Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2x82","depends_on_id":"br-3ddq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:47:15.599233379Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2x82","depends_on_id":"br-3lyw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:47:15.149689611Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2x82","depends_on_id":"br-3rls","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:47:12.084989800Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2x82","depends_on_id":"br-84gq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:47:13.833226117Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2x82","depends_on_id":"br-h1yu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:47:14.270479001Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
